description_long: This is a simple, utilitarian fence constructed from four vertical posts and two horizontal rails. The posts are rectangular in cross-section and evenly spaced, with the rails attached in a staggered, slightly offset manner, giving the fence a casual, unfinished look. The structure appears to be made of wood or a wood-like material, with a matte, painted or stained finish in a light tan color. There are no visible decorative elements, caps, or embellishments on the posts or rails, emphasizing its functional purpose as a barrier. The top edge of the fence is flat, with the rails not perfectly aligned, adding to its rustic appearance. This type of fence is commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as a temporary barrier on sidewalks or construction sites.
description: A simple, tan wooden fence with four posts and two staggered horizontal rails, featuring a flat top and no decorative elements. Its utilitarian design is suitable for urban parks or as a temporary barrier.
description_view_0: Front view showing four vertical posts and two staggered horizontal rails, with the offset alignment clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence and the depth of the rails and posts.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the same four posts and two rails, but with the staggered alignment less pronounced.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the thickness of the rails and posts.
